Course Title: AI in Safety-Critical Transport Systems: What Can Go Wrong?
Category: Transportation & Logistics: Aviation, Maritime, Logistics, Shipping, Automotive
Who is this for: Transport managers, logistics coordinators, fleet managers, safety officers, operations managers, supply chain managers, aviation personnel, maritime operators, automotive industry professionals, regulatory compliance staff
Team or Dept this is for: Transportation, Logistics, Operations, Safety, Compliance, Fleet Management, Supply Chain
Description: How AI errors can directly impact passenger and cargo safety.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with how transport systems operate (such as aviation, road, or maritime) and a general curiosity about technology are all you need to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify common AI failure modes in safety-critical transport systems (including aviation, maritime, and automotive applications) and explain how these errors can directly threaten passenger and cargo safety. You will also be able to apply a structured risk awareness framework to recognise early warning signs of AI-related hazards and describe the human oversight measures used to mitigate them.
